import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

class Config:
    SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY", "dev-secret-change-me")
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")
    SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS = False
    CELERY_BROKER_URL = os.environ.get("REDIS_URL", "redis://localhost:6379/0")
    CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND = os.environ.get("REDIS_URL", "redis://localhost:6379/0")
    STRIPE_SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("STRIPE_SECRET_KEY")
    FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH = os.environ.get("FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH")
    MAX_POINTS_MULTIPLIER = 3.0

class DevelopmentConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = True

class ProductionConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = False

config = {
    "development": DevelopmentConfig,
    "production": ProductionConfig,
    "default": DevelopmentConfig,
}

from app.extensions import db
from sqlalchemy import Index

class PromotionsConfig(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = "promotions_config"

    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    multiplier = db.Column(db.Float, nullable=False, default=1.0)
    active = db.Column(db.Boolean, nullable=False, default=False)
    label = db.Column(db.String(128), nullable=True)
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, server_default=db.func.now())
    updated_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, server_default=db.func.now(), onupdate=db.func.now())

    __table_args__ = (
        Index("ix_promotions_config_active", "active"),
    )

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<PromotionsConfig id={self.id} multiplier={self.multiplier} active={self.active}>"

from celery import shared_task
from app.utils.firebase import send_push_notification
from app.models.order import Order
from app.models.user import User

@shared_task
def notify_order_ready(order_id):
    order = Order.query.get(order_id)
    if order is None:
        return

    user = User.query.get(order.user_id)
    if user is None or not user.fcm_token:
        return

    send_push_notification(
        token=user.fcm_token,
        title="Your order is ready!",
        body=f"Order #{order.id} is ready for pickup.",
    )

import firebase_admin
from firebase_admin import credentials, messaging
import os

_app = None

def _get_app():
    global _app
    if _app is None:
        cred = credentials.Certificate(os.environ["FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS_PATH"])
        _app = firebase_admin.initialize_app(cred)
    return _app

def send_push_notification(token: str, title: str, body: str):
    _get_app()
    message = messaging.Message(
        notification=messaging.Notification(title=title, body=body),
        token=token,
    )
    response = messaging.send(message)
    return response

from app.extensions import db
import uuid

class User(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id = db.Column(db.UUID(as_uuid=True), primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)
    email = db.Column(db.String(255), unique=True, nullable=False)
    phone = db.Column(db.String(20), nullable=True)
    rewards_points = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0, nullable=False)
    fcm_token = db.Column(db.String(512), nullable=True)
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, server_default=db.func.now())

    orders = db.relationship("Order", back_populates="user", lazy="dynamic")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User {self.email}>"

from app.extensions import db
import uuid

class Order(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = "orders"

    id = db.Column(db.UUID(as_uuid=True), primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)
    user_id = db.Column(db.UUID(as_uuid=True), db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    status = db.Column(db.String(50), nullable=False, default="pending")
    total_cents = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False)
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, server_default=db.func.now())
    updated_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, server_default=db.func.now(), onupdate=db.func.now())

    user = db.relationship("User", back_populates="orders")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order {self.id} status={self.status}>"

from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify
from app.extensions import db
from app.models.order import Order
from app.tasks.notifications import notify_order_ready
from app.tasks.rewards import apply_rewards_points

orders_bp = Blueprint("orders", __name__, url_prefix="/orders")

@orders_bp.route("/<order_id>/status", methods=["PATCH"])
def update_order_status(order_id):
    order = Order.query.get_or_404(order_id)
    data = request.get_json()
    new_status = data.get("status")

    if not new_status:
        return jsonify({"error": "status is required"}), 400

    order.status = new_status
    db.session.commit()

    if new_status == "ready":
        notify_order_ready.delay(str(order.id))

    if new_status == "completed":
        apply_rewards_points.delay(str(order.id))

    return jsonify({"id": str(order.id), "status": order.status}), 200

"""add promotions_config table

Revision ID: a3f891bc2201
Revises: 002_add_rewards_points
Create Date: 2024-03-28 14:07:33.412891

"""
from alembic import op
import sqlalchemy as sa

revision = "a3f891bc2201"
down_revision = "002_add_rewards_points"
branch_labels = None
depends_on = None

def upgrade():
    op.create_table(
        "promotions_config",
        sa.Column("id", sa.Integer(), nullable=False),
        sa.Column("multiplier", sa.Float(), nullable=False, server_default="1.0"),
        sa.Column("active", sa.Boolean(), nullable=False, server_default="false"),
        sa.Column("label", sa.String(length=128), nullable=True),
        sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(), server_default=sa.func.now()),
        sa.Column("updated_at", sa.DateTime(), server_default=sa.func.now()),
        sa.PrimaryKeyConstraint("id"),
    )
    op.create_index("ix_promotions_config_active", "promotions_config", ["active"])

def downgrade():
    op.drop_index("ix_promotions_config_active", table_name="promotions_config")
    op.drop_table("promotions_config")

import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from app.tasks.rewards import apply_rewards_points, get_active_multiplier

def test_get_active_multiplier_no_config(app):
    with app.app_context():
        with patch("app.tasks.rewards.PromotionsConfig") as MockConfig:
            MockConfig.query.filter_by.return_value.first.return_value = None
            result = get_active_multiplier()
            assert result == 1.0

def test_get_active_multiplier_respects_cap(app):
    with app.app_context():
        mock_config = MagicMock()
        mock_config.multiplier = 10.0
        with patch("app.tasks.rewards.PromotionsConfig") as MockConfig:
            MockConfig.query.filter_by.return_value.first.return_value = mock_config
            result = get_active_multiplier()
            assert result == 5.0

def test_apply_rewards_weekend(app, db_session, sample_order):
    with patch("app.tasks.rewards.datetime") as mock_dt:
        mock_dt.utcnow.return_value.weekday.return_value = 5
        with patch("app.tasks.rewards.get_active_multiplier", return_value=2.0):
            apply_rewards_points(str(sample_order.id))
            assert sample_order.user.rewards_points == int(sample_order.total_cents / 100) * 2

import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from app.tasks.notifications import notify_order_ready

def test_notify_order_ready_sends(app, db_session, sample_order):
    sample_order.user.fcm_token = "fake-device-token-abc123"
    with patch("app.tasks.notifications.send_push_notification") as mock_send:
        notify_order_ready(str(sample_order.id))
        mock_send.assert_called_once()
        call_kwargs = mock_send.call_args[1]
        assert call_kwargs["token"] == "fake-device-token-abc123"

def test_notify_order_ready_no_token(app, db_session, sample_order):
    sample_order.user.fcm_token = None
    with patch("app.tasks.notifications.send_push_notification") as mock_send:
        notify_order_ready(str(sample_order.id))
        mock_send.assert_not_called()

def test_notify_order_ready_bad_id(app):
    with patch("app.tasks.notifications.send_push_notification") as mock_send:
        notify_order_ready("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000")
        mock_send.assert_not_called()

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, staging]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: brewgo
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: brewgo
          POSTGRES_DB: brewgo_test
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5

      redis:
        image: redis:6.2
        ports:
          - 6379:6379

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run migrations
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://brewgo:brewgo@localhost:5432/brewgo_test
        run: flask db upgrade

      - name: Run tests
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://brewgo:brewgo@localhost:5432/brewgo_test
          REDIS_URL: redis://localhost:6379/0
          FLASK_ENV: development
        run: pytest --tb=short -q

name: Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy-staging:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy to Elastic Beanstalk (staging)
        uses: einaregilsson/beanstalk-deploy@v21
        with:
          aws_access_key: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws_secret_key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          application_name: brewgo-api
          environment_name: brewgo-api-staging
          region: us-east-1
          version_label: ${{ github.sha }}
          deployment_package: deploy.zip

  deploy-production:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: deploy-staging
    environment: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy to Elastic Beanstalk (production)
        uses: einaregilsson/beanstalk-deploy@v21
        with:
          aws_access_key: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws_secret_key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          application_name: brewgo-api
          environment_name: brewgo-api-production
          region: us-east-1
          version_label: ${{ github.sha }}
          deployment_package: deploy.zip

pull_requests.title: Add promotions_config table and rewards multiplier Celery task
pull_requests.head: feature/brw-44-loyalty-multiplier
pull_requests.author: daniel.cho
pull_requests.body:
Closes BRW-44.

Adds the `promotions_config` table via Alembic migration and wires up the `apply_rewards_points` Celery task to read from it. When `active = true` on a config row and the day is Saturday or Sunday, the multiplier is applied to the points calculation.

I've got the cap set to 5x in `rewards.py` for now — still need to confirm with product what the actual limit should be. Will update before merge.

Tests are in `test_rewards.py`, covering the no-config case, the cap enforcement, and a weekend multiplier scenario.

commits.message: add promotions_config migration
implement get_active_multiplier and apply_rewards_points task
add tests for rewards multiplier logic
wire apply_rewards_points into order completion route

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: Migration looks good. Can you double-check the index is getting created correctly on CI? I want to make sure `ix_promotions_config_active` shows up in the test DB.

comments.author: daniel.cho
comments.body: Yeah just checked the CI logs, index is there. All green.

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: The cap should be 3x per what Marcus relayed from the product meeting. Can you update `MAX_MULTIPLIER` in `rewards.py`? Also should probably live in `config.py` rather than hardcoded in the task file.

comments.author: daniel.cho
comments.body: On it — will move it to config and update the value.

pull_requests.title: Fix FCM token lookup in notify_order_ready task
pull_requests.head: fix/brw-45-push-notification
pull_requests.author: daniel.cho
pull_requests.body:
Related to BRW-45.

The `notify_order_ready` task was calling `send_push_notification` before confirming the user record actually had an `fcm_token` set. In cases where the token was null, the Firebase SDK was throwing an unhandled exception that Celery was silently retrying until it hit max retries, which is why the notification never reached the device but the backend logs showed the call going out.

Also noticed the `fcm_token` column didn't exist on the `users` table yet — added it in a separate migration. This needs its own migration PR but including it here so review can see the full picture.

commits.message: add fcm_token column to users table
guard fcm_token before calling firebase send
add tests for notify_order_ready task

comments.author: marcus.webb
comments.body: This makes sense. One thing — should we be logging when we skip the notification because `fcm_token` is null? Would help with debugging future reports.

comments.author: daniel.cho
comments.body: Good call, added a `logger.warning` in the null token branch.

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: The `fcm_token` migration needs its own version file in `migrations/versions/` before this merges, not a standalone script. Can you move it over?

comments.author: daniel.cho
comments.body: Done, moved it to `004_add_fcm_token_to_users.py`. Will push in the next commit.

pull_requests.title: Upgrade Python runtime to 3.11.4 and update Procfile
pull_requests.head: chore/python-311-upgrade
pull_requests.author: priya.nair
pull_requests.body: Bumps `runtime.txt` to `python-3.11.4` and adjusts the Procfile worker count from 2 to 4 to take advantage of the performance headroom. Also pinned a few packages in `requirements.txt` that were loose.

commits.message: bump runtime.txt to python-3.11.4
update Procfile worker count
pin flask and sqlalchemy versions in requirements.txt

comments.author: marcus.webb
comments.body: Looks fine, staging seemed happy after the push. The weirdness I saw this morning must have been a cache thing.

comments.author: daniel.cho
comments.body: LGTM. One note — do we want to update the CI redis version while we're in here? I've been on 7 locally.

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: Keeping CI on 6.2 for now to match staging. I'll do a separate PR for the redis bump after BRW-38 ships.

pull_requests.title: Add order status index migration
pull_requests.head: chore/order-status-index
pull_requests.author: priya.nair
pull_requests.body: Adding an index on `orders.status` — query times on the kitchen dashboard were climbing as order volume grows. Simple migration, no model changes needed since SQLAlchemy reflects the index automatically.

commits.message: add index on orders.status

comments.author: daniel.cho
comments.body: Nice. Did you check the EXPLAIN output before and after locally?

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: Yeah, seq scan dropped to index scan on the status filter queries. p95 on that endpoint was sitting around 320ms, expecting it to come down significantly.

commits.message:
add promotions_config migration

Creates the promotions_config table with multiplier (float), active (boolean), label (varchar), and timestamps. Adds index on active column per Priya's note in Slack.

Ref: BRW-44

implement get_active_multiplier and apply_rewards_points task

Reads from promotions_config when active=True, applies multiplier on weekend orders. Cap is at 5x for now, need to confirm with product.

Ref: BRW-44

guard fcm_token before calling firebase send

The task was passing None to the Firebase SDK when fcm_token wasn't set, causing an unhandled exception that Celery retried silently. Added null check and early return. Added logger.warning for the null token case per Marcus's review comment.

Ref: BRW-45

bump runtime.txt to python-3.11.4

Elastic Beanstalk platform update supports 3.11.4, moving off 3.10.12.

add index on orders.status

Kitchen dashboard queries filtering by status were doing sequential scans. This should bring p95 latency down on that endpoint.

No model changes required.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, staging]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint

pull_requests.title: Update OrderStatus component to handle new "cancelled" status
pull_requests.head: feature/order-cancelled-status
pull_requests.author: marcus.webb
pull_requests.body:
Product wants to surface a "Cancelled" state in the UI for orders that get rejected (e.g., item out of stock). Added `cancelled` to the `STATUS_LABELS` and `STATUS_COLORS` maps in `OrderStatus.jsx` and updated the test file to cover it.

Also noticed the `RewardsScreen` copy still says "2x points every weekend" but BRW-44 is introducing a variable multiplier. Leaving that for a follow-up once the backend is confirmed — just flagging here.

commits.message: add cancelled status to OrderStatus component
update OrderStatus tests for cancelled state

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: LGTM on the component changes. Agree the rewards copy needs updating — can you open a ticket so it doesn't get lost?

comments.author: marcus.webb
comments.body: Yep, opened BRW-47 for it.

pull_requests.title: Register FCM token on app launch
pull_requests.head: feature/brw-45-fcm-registration
pull_requests.author: marcus.webb
pull_requests.body:
Part of the BRW-45 investigation. We found that a lot of users never had their FCM token stored because `registerForPushNotifications` was only being called on the permissions prompt screen, which users who'd already granted permissions in a previous version never saw again.

Moving the registration call to app startup so it refreshes the token each launch. Also wires up the `configureNotificationHandler` call that was missing from `App.js`.

commits.message: call registerForPushNotifications on app mount
add configureNotificationHandler to App.js
add /users/me/fcm-token endpoint to api client

comments.author: daniel.cho
comments.body: This probably explains a big chunk of the missing notifications. Good catch.

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: The backend endpoint `/users/me/fcm-token` doesn't exist yet — Daniel's PR #17 adds it. Make sure these two merge in the right order or CI will catch weird failures.

comments.author: marcus.webb
comments.body: Got it, will merge #17 first.

pull_requests.title: Upgrade react-navigation to v6 across the board
pull_requests.head: chore/react-nav-v6
pull_requests.author: marcus.webb
pull_requests.body: We were mixing v4 and v6 packages which was causing some deprecation warnings and one actual crash on Android when deep-linking into the order status screen. Cleaned everything up to v6. Stack navigator API change required updates to `AppNavigator.jsx`.

commits.message: remove react-navigation v4 package
update AppNavigator to v6 stack API
update package.json nav dependencies
fix deep link config for order status screen

comments.author: daniel.cho
comments.body: Good. I noticed the v4 package was still listed in package.json even after you'd installed v6 a while back. Was that causing actual runtime issues or just the crash you mentioned?

comments.author: marcus.webb
comments.body: Both. The deep link crash was the v4/v6 API mismatch. The leftover package.json entry meant anyone doing a fresh `npm ci` was pulling in both.

commits.message:
add cancelled status to OrderStatus component

Adds label and color for "cancelled" order state. Backend is already returning this status in some edge cases, UI was just showing the raw string.

call registerForPushNotifications on app mount

Was only running on the permissions screen which existing users never hit again. Moving to app startup so token stays fresh.

Ref: BRW-45

remove react-navigation v4 package

Was conflicting with v6 install. Deep link crash on Android traced back to API mismatch between the two.

files.content:
resource "aws_db_instance" "postgres" {
  identifier              = "${var.app_name}-postgres"
  engine                  = "postgres"
  engine_version          = "15.5"
  instance_class          = var.db_instance_class
  db_name                 = var.db_name
  username                = var.db_username
  password                = var.db_password
  allocated_storage       = 20
  max_allocated_storage   = 100
  storage_encrypted       = true
  backup_retention_period = 7
  deletion_protection     = true
  skip_final_snapshot     = false
  final_snapshot_identifier = "${var.app_name}-final-snapshot"

  tags = {
    Project     = "brewgo"
    Environment = var.environment
  }
}

# brewgo-infra

Terraform configs and utility scripts for the Brew & Go infrastructure on AWS.

## What's in here

- `terraform/` — All infrastructure defined as code. We use a module structure for RDS, Elastic Beanstalk, and ElastiCache.
- `scripts/` — Utility scripts for DB snapshots and secret rotation.

## Prerequisites

- Terraform >= 1.7
- AWS CLI configured with appropriate credentials
- Access to the `brewgo-infra` IAM role

## Environments

We run two environments: `staging` and `production`. Both are defined via workspace in Terraform.

```
terraform workspace select staging
terraform plan
terraform apply
```

## Notes

- Redis is on 6.2 in both environments. A 7.x upgrade is planned but not scheduled.
- RDS is PostgreSQL 15. Automated backups retain for 7 days.
- EB solution stack targets Python 3.11. Update `eb_solution_stack` in `variables.tf` if the platform version changes.

pull_requests.title: Add ElastiCache Redis module to Terraform
pull_requests.head: infra/add-redis-module
pull_requests.author: priya.nair
pull_requests.body:
Moves the Redis cluster config out of the root `main.tf` and into a proper module under `modules/redis/`. No infrastructure changes, just a refactor so it's consistent with how we handle RDS and EB.

Pinned the engine version to `6.2.14` explicitly rather than relying on the AWS default for that minor version.

commits.message: extract redis into terraform module
pin redis engine version to 6.2.14
update root main.tf to call redis module

comments.author: daniel.cho
comments.body: Makes sense. Should we document somewhere that we're deliberately on 6.x? I've seen a few comments about bumping to 7.

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: Good idea. I'll add a note in the README.

pull_requests.title: Increase RDS allocated storage and enable encryption
pull_requests.head: infra/rds-storage-encryption
pull_requests.author: priya.nair
pull_requests.body:
Two changes: bumping allocated storage from 10GB to 20GB (we were at ~78% on production) and enabling `storage_encrypted`. The encryption change required a brief outage window — coordinated with the team offline, done during low-traffic hours Sunday night.

Also flipped `deletion_protection` to `true` since that should have been on from the start.

commits.message: increase rds storage and enable encryption

comments.author: marcus.webb
comments.body: Thanks for handling this. The 78% was making me nervous.

commits.message:
extract redis into terraform module

Consistent with rds and elasticbeanstalk modules. No changes to actual infrastructure config, just reorganizing.

increase rds storage and enable encryption

Allocated storage: 10 -> 20GB
Max allocated storage: 100GB (autoscaling headroom)
storage_encrypted: true
deletion_protection: true

Outage window: 2024-01-21 02:00-02:40 UTC
